## Determinism

- Three complete Parquet builds from the same pinned receipt and normalization configuration produced identical file and logical-table SHA-256 values.
- Independent full runs produced the same ingestion run ID `hebrew-7db8035c6ae1c3268074` and the same 475,911-row token table.
- The logical token-table SHA-256 was `fafa37f26abaf2156baa92338194e92e7d39ddbf781e082c67cbac3ace2ceef0` on every full run.
- The Parquet token-file SHA-256 was `c49e0c0b20b2f93afe377fcd78654318382ba6c2bac5f3b4e09c765820a9f1a3` on every full run.
- DuckDB row counts and logical fingerprints matched the Parquet artifacts after transactional reload; reruns introduced no duplicate rows.
- A reordered-input fixture test confirms canonical identifiers and logical output are independent of filesystem enumeration order.

## Local environment

- Python: `3.12.13`
- Operating system: `Windows 11`
- Machine: `AMD64`

## Known limitations

- The selected node representation has no formal XSD; the adapter pins and validates the observed structure.
- MACULA normally prefers Qere where present, so separate Ketiv material is not complete in this representation.
- Upstream issues document occasional missing xml:id values, split-word identifier inconsistencies, and a reported 1 Chronicles node-numbering problem; all are surfaced as validation findings.
- Release 25.08.11 predates later NFC and combining-grapheme-joiner fixes; Project Echoes applies Unicode normalization only to derived forms and preserves source values.
- The aggregate combines components with distinct attribution and downstream-publication conditions.

## Acceptance gate

The Milestone 2 acceptance gate passed.
